skill.name: data-engineering-data-driven-feature
skill.description: Build features guided by data insights, A/B testing, and continuous measurement using specialized agents for analysis, implementation, and experimentation.
skill.summary: Data-driven feature development workflow using A/B testing, analytics instrumentation, and statistical analysis for measurable outcomes.
skill.author: sickn33

security_audit.summary: All 16 static findings are false positives. The skill is a legitimate data engineering workflow orchestrating specialized agents for data analysis, hypothesis development, A/B testing, and statistical analysis. No external commands, cryptographic algorithms, or malicious code patterns are present.

high_findings.title: Weak Cryptographic Algorithm Detection (False Positive)
high_findings.description: Static scanner incorrectly flagged 'Bayesian', 'LaunchDarkly', 'Snowflake', 'BigQuery', and similar terms as weak cryptographic algorithms. These are legitimate statistical methods (Bayesian statistics) and cloud services (feature flags, data warehouses). No cryptographic code exists in this file.
high_findings.confidence_reasoning: The flagged terms are clearly legitimate data engineering concepts (Bayesian A/B testing, LaunchDarkly feature flags, Snowflake data warehouse) and not cryptographic algorithms. No cryptographic code is present.

medium_findings.title: External Command Execution Detection (False Positive)
medium_findings.description: Static scanner detected backticks and flagged as shell command execution. These are markdown code fences and inline code references in documentation, not actual shell commands.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: Line 29 contains markdown inline code (backticks around 'resources/implementation-playbook.md'). Lines 140-163 contain a YAML configuration code block. Neither executes shell commands.

content.user_title: Build Data-Driven Features with A/B Testing
content.value_statement: This skill provides a comprehensive workflow for building features guided by data insights, A/B testing, and continuous measurement using specialized AI agents for analysis, implementation, and experimentation.
actual_capabilities: Orchestrate specialized agents for exploratory data analysis and hypothesis development
Design statistical experiments with proper sample size calculation and power analysis
Create analytics instrumentation plans using Segment, Amplitude, or Mixpanel
Implement feature flags with gradual rollout strategies using LaunchDarkly or Split.io
Perform statistical analysis of A/B test results with confidence intervals
Configure real-time monitoring dashboards for experiment metrics
limitations: Does not execute actual data analysis or build code - provides guidance and prompts only
Requires external analytics platforms (Amplitude, Mixpanel, Segment) to be configured separately
Does not have access to your actual data or metrics - generates plans and prompts
Statistical recommendations are starting points that require domain expert validation

use_cases.title: Product Manager Planning Experiments
use_cases.description: Product managers use this workflow to plan data-driven feature launches with proper experiment design, success metrics definition, and statistical rigor.
use_cases.target_user: Product managers, growth engineers, and data-informed decision makers

use_cases.title: Data Scientist Structuring Analysis
use_cases.description: Data scientists follow this workflow to structure A/B tests, define hypotheses with ICE or RICE scoring, and perform proper statistical analysis.

use_cases.title: Engineer Implementing Feature Flags
use_cases.description: Backend engineers use this workflow to implement feature flags, configure gradual rollouts, and set up proper analytics instrumentation from the start.

prompt_templates.title: Start Data-Driven Feature
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the data-engineering-data-driven-feature skill to help me plan a new feature. The feature is: [describe your feature idea]. I want to validate it with A/B testing and measure its impact on [key business metric].
prompt_templates.scenario: Initial planning phase for a new feature

prompt_templates.title: Design Experiment
prompt_templates.prompt: I need to design an A/B test for [feature name]. Help me create a statistical experiment design including sample size calculation, success metrics, and guardrail metrics. Target metric is [metric], expected improvement is [percentage].

prompt_templates.title: Plan Analytics Instrumentation
prompt_templates.prompt: Help me design comprehensive analytics instrumentation for [feature]. I need to track user interactions including [list events]. Recommend event schemas and properties for segmentation in [Amplitude/Mixpanel/Segment].

prompt_templates.title: Analyze Test Results
prompt_templates.prompt: Our A/B test for [feature] has completed with [sample size] users over [time period]. Treatment showed [results]. Help me perform statistical analysis, calculate significance, and assess business impact.

output_examples.input: Start data-driven feature for a new checkout flow optimization
output_examples.output: Workflow plan: (1) EDA phase - analyze current conversion funnel, (2) Hypothesis - 'Simplified checkout will increase conversion by 5%', (3) Experiment design - 2-week test, 10% traffic, primary metric: checkout completion rate, (4) Analytics - track checkout_start, checkout_step_complete, checkout_abandon, (5) Architecture - LaunchDarkly feature flag, gradual rollout starting at 5%

output_examples.input: Design experiment for recommendation algorithm change
output_examples.output: Statistical design: Required sample size 50,000 users per variant for 80% power to detect 2% improvement. Primary metric: click-through rate, Guardrail metrics: latency, error rate. Randomization: user_id hash. Analysis: two-tailed t-test with Bonferroni correction for multiple metrics.

best_practices: Define success metrics and guardrail metrics before starting development to avoid p-hacking
Start gradual rollouts at 5-10% traffic and monitor error rates before increasing
Run experiments for full weekly cycles to account for day-of-week effects
Document all decisions and learnings for future feature development cycles
anti_patterns: Running experiments without predefined success criteria and making decisions ad-hoc
Starting with full traffic rollout instead of gradual rollout risking widespread issues
Ignoring guardrail metrics and deploying changes that hurt user experience
Ending experiments early when results look promising without proper statistical power

faq.question: What AI tools support this skill?
faq.answer: This skill works with Claude, Codex, and Claude Code. It provides prompts and workflows that these AI tools execute to help with data-driven development.

faq.question: Do I need external analytics platforms?
faq.answer: Yes, this skill recommends platforms like Amplitude, Mixpanel, Segment, LaunchDarkly, or Split.io. These must be configured separately in your infrastructure.

faq.question: Can this skill analyze my actual data?
faq.answer: No, this skill generates guidance, prompts, and plans. It does not have access to your data systems. You will need to execute the generated plans with your data team.

faq.question: How long should I run an A/B test?
faq.answer: Tests should run for at least one full weekly cycle (7 days) to account for day-of-week variation. Most experiments run 1-4 weeks depending on required sample size.

faq.question: What statistical methods are used?
faq.answer: The skill supports both frequentist (t-tests, chi-square) and Bayesian approaches. Bayesian methods can provide faster decision-making with probabilistic interpretations.

faq.question: Is this suitable for ML model A/B testing?
faq.answer: Yes, Phase 9 covers ML model integration including online inference, A/B testing between model versions, performance tracking, and drift detection.
